name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
description: Free 24/7 crisis support via call or text. Dial or text 988.

name: Crisis Text Line
description: Text HOME to 741741 for free, confidential 24/7 crisis counseling via text message.

name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: Free 24/7 crisis support for veterans and their families. Call 988 and press 1, or text 838255.

name: Trans Lifeline
description: Peer support hotline run by and for trans people. Call 877-565-8860.

name: The Trevor Project
description: Crisis intervention and suicide prevention for LGBTQ+ young people. Call 1-866-488-7386.

name: NAMI (National Alliance on Mental Illness)
description: Education, peer support groups, advocacy, and a helpline for people affected by mental illness.

name: Mental Health America
description: Online mental health screening tools, resources, and peer advocacy for everyone.

name: 7 Cups
description: Free anonymous online chat with trained listeners, available 24/7 for emotional support.

name: Open Path Collective
description: Affordable therapy at $30–$80 per session from licensed therapists for individuals, couples, and children.

name: PsychCentral
description: Mental health articles, quizzes, and news reviewed by licensed professionals.

name: SAMHSA National Helpline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referral and information service. Call 1-800-662-4357.

name: SAMHSA Disaster Distress Helpline
description: 24/7 crisis counseling for people experiencing distress after a disaster. Call 1-800-985-5990.

name: Project Reach
description: Free peer support via chat or text — real humans, no judgment, no waitlist. Text +1 (901) 492-1712.

name: ADAA (Anxiety & Depression Association of America)
description: Find therapists, self-help tools, and research-backed resources for anxiety, depression, and related conditions.

name: Headspace
description: Guided meditation and mindfulness exercises for stress relief and better sleep. Free basics available.

name: Calm
description: Meditation, sleep stories, and breathing exercises to manage stress and anxiety. Free trial available.

name: Depression and Bipolar Support Alliance (DBSA)
description: Free in-person and online support groups for people living with depression or bipolar disorder.

name: Families for Depression Awareness
description: Resources and support for families and caregivers of people with depression or bipolar disorder.

name: Alcoholics Anonymous (AA)
description: Free worldwide peer support for people recovering from alcohol use disorder.

name: Narcotics Anonymous (NA)
description: Free worldwide peer support for people recovering from addiction to drugs.

name: SMART Recovery
description: Free science-based addiction support meetings online and in-person, focused on self-empowerment.
